behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels for attendee attribution.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies because correctness depends on real speech-model diarization output quality; a deterministic stub cannot expose mislabeled/merged speakers. REAL-INFRA alone (System) is insufficient as the core risk is model judgment, not transport. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: a System-level test with fixed transcript fixtures (or mocked STT body) passes, while real STT on overlapping voices drops speaker labels and attribution fails. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior text targets one model decision boundary (STT speaker labeling), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio with multiple speakers through the real STT API, then assert transcript speaker tags remain distinct and map to known attendees for downstream attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS indicates this is parser logic at a local function/object boundary: extract structured assignment fields from transcript text. No required OS/network/subprocess, and no dependence on AI output quality. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text is specific to parsing extraction logic, not cross-module state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript snippets into the assignment parser and assert exact structured outputs (assignee, task text, spans) including edge cases like multiple assignees and malformed lines.

behaviors.description: Live summary output includes at least one action item for each attendee assigned a task.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM generation decisions (coverage/completeness per attendee), so LLM-DEP upgrades beyond Integration. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: an Integration test with a canned LLM response always containing all attendees passes, while real model output omits one attendee’s action item despite assignment present in transcript. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because this behavior can be validated at a single summarization decision boundary; it does not require validating multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Provide real transcript inputs with known per-attendee assignments to the live summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into `decisions` schema instead of narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior describes deterministic routing/mapping logic after decisions are already identified. Failure boundary is local transformation logic, not infra or model quality. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text focuses on field routing semantics, not module interaction-dependent failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the routing function with inputs containing pre-identified decision objects and assert outputs place them only in `decisions` schema fields, never narrative containers.

behaviors.description: Key decisions in transcript appear in live summary `decisions` section, not buried in narrative.
behaviors.justification: This validates model output placement/quality, not just routing code. A stubbed model would hide failures where real generation mentions decisions only narratively. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: Integration with fixed model fixture placing decisions correctly passes, while real model outputs decision text in prose and leaves `decisions` sparse. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because the behavior can be assessed at the single live summarization output decision, without requiring multi-agentic stage sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcript samples containing explicit decisions through live summarization and assert each expected decision is represented in structured `decisions` entries rather than only freeform narrative text.

behaviors.description: For audio >60 minutes, service segments audio, processes sequentially, and merges into one coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP applies: repeated segment-level transcribe/summarize decisions plus merge/coherence across steps. LLM/STT outputs at each segment can alter downstream merge correctness, so single-step Agentic is insufficient. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: an Agentic test on one long segment with one model call passes, but real multi-segment run produces conflicting action items/decisions after sequential merge due to cross-segment drift. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because behavior text explicitly requires sequence + merge over multiple segments.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real >60-minute meeting audio through production segmentation pipeline; verify segment count/order, sequential processing, and final merged summary consistency (deduplicated actions, preserved decisions, stable attendee attribution across segments).

behaviors.description: Full pipeline (upload, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) runs end-to-end on real meeting recording without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit end-to-end orchestration with multiple agentic steps (STT then LLM summarization) plus delivery handoffs. Failures often emerge from stage-to-stage state propagation and autonomous progression. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: an Agentic test of only summarization succeeds, while full run fails when transcription format from real audio breaks downstream parser/delivery contract. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because behavior text explicitly spans the whole pipeline sequence, not one model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real recording through production entrypoint (upload to delivered structured artifact), assert no human intervention, successful stage transitions, and valid final schema containing action items, decisions, and attributions.
